ABCD.name: ABCD (Ray transfer) matrix
ABCD.description: Paraxial ray transfer (2x2) used for Gaussian beam/ray propagation.
ABCD.when_to_use: Paraxial systems: thin lenses, free space, mirrors (small angles).
ABCD.example: Free space L: [[1, L], [0, 1]]; Thin lens f: [[1,0],[-1/f,1]]
ABCD.caveats: Not valid for large angles or strongly diffractive systems.

Jones.name: Jones matrix (2x2 complex)
Jones.description: Represents polarization effects for coherent light.
Jones.caveats: Only for fully polarized, coherent light.

Mueller.name: Mueller matrix (4x4 real)
Mueller.description: Represents polarization effects for partially polarized light.
Mueller.when_to_use: Use for depolarizing systems and intensity-level polarization measurements.
Mueller.caveats: Larger and less expressive for coherent phase.

Fourier.name: Fourier transform (discrete matrix)
Fourier.description: Transforms spatial to frequency domain; used in diffraction and imaging.
Fourier.caveats: Sampling, zero-padding, and aliasing must be managed.
